definition: Animals seized or confiscated by authorities (peace officers, humane society officers, animal control officers, or law enforcement) for legal reasons and housed at RCDAS for a period mandated by state or local law. Animals may be owned or stray. See Penal Code 597.1. Use when legal authority removes an animal rather than voluntary surrender or routine stray pickup.
synonyms: seizure; seized animal; impound by police
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal intentionally left by its owner without providing care or support. Example: Dog chained in vacant lot with no food or water; owner identified but refused to reclaim.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal confiscated by an animal control officer from a contract city (sheltering only). Example: Contract city officer seizes animal for hoarding case.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal confiscated due to bite incident involving another person or another animal. Example: Dog bites neighbor; confiscated for quarantine and investigation.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal confiscated due to cruelty or neglect. Example: Severely emaciated horses seized by authorities.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal confiscated when owner is evicted from property. Example: Cats left behind after landlord eviction.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal seized when owner is hospitalized and unable to arrange care. Example: Owner has medical emergency; no family available.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal confiscated due to death of owner. Example: Deceased person's animal discovered by authorities; no next of kin.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Police confiscated animal and brought to shelter. Example: Police at domestic violence call find injured animals.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal confiscated due to threat of public safety. Example: Aggressive dog repeatedly escaping yard, chasing children.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Special circumstances such as disaster response (fire, flooding, earthquake). Example: Wildfire evacuation; animals rescued from danger zone.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Dead animal brought to RCDAS for disposal. Subtypes: OTC (by public), FIELD (by field officer).
synonyms: dead animal dropoff; carcass disposal; deceased animal
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Owner requests humane euthanasia due to medical or behavioral issue. Subtypes: BITE (after bite incident), FIELD (surrendered to officer in field), OTC (brought to shelter), HOSPITAL (owner hospitalized, no one to care for pet).
synonyms: owner requested euthanasia; put down request; owner-intended euthanasia; ORE
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Healthy Pet Zone program providing veterinary care, supplies, and support to help families keep their pets during difficult times. Example: homeless person hospitalized with no one to care for their pet.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal voluntarily brought to the shelter and relinquished by its owner, including adoption returns. Use when the owner is making a conscious decision to give up the animal.
synonyms: dropped off; gave up pet; relinquished; surrender
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Owner surrendered animal more than 30 days after adoption. Example: Cat adopted 2 months ago returned for behavior issues.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Custodian voluntarily turned in animal citing abandonment as the reason. Example: Landlord requests RCDAS take animal left behind by tenant.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Owner surrendered to animal control officer from contract city. Example: Contract city officer accepts surrender and delivers animal to RCDAS.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Owner voluntarily surrendered animal after it bit another person or animal. Example: Dog bites postal worker; owner surrenders voluntarily.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Owner voluntarily surrenders animal after or just prior to an officer seizing the animal for a cited animal cruelty violation. Example: Chained dog without shelter/food/water; owner agrees to surrender.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Owner surrendered animal directly to an officer in the field rather than at the shelter. Example: Officer responds to loose dog call; owner admits they cannot care for it and surrenders in field.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Owner surrendered animal at shelter counter (Over the Counter). Example: Family moving to apartment that does not allow pets.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal adopted from RCDAS is returned to the shelter. Use when the owner is returning an animal adopted from an RCDAS shelter.
synonyms: adoption return; bring back adopted pet
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Owner returns adopted animal due to bite incident. Example: family adopts puppy, it bites child, family brings it back.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Adopted animal returned within 30 days. Example: dog adopted December 15, returned December 17.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: FOR BILLING PURPOSES ONLY. Used to document and bill contract cities. Not counted in live release rate.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)
notes: Billing artifact; see edit log data-integrity note on Unredeemed Owned Animals SOP

definition: Animal stated to be unowned, free-roaming, or found without owner present. Held for legally mandated holding period to allow owner reclaim. Use when found at large or ownership unknown at intake.
synonyms: found animal; loose dog; loose cat; lost pet
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Stray animal running at large, brought in by member of public.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Stray picked up by animal control officer from contract city.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Stray brought to shelter by public due to bite incident (quarantine).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Dead stray animal brought to shelter (not counted in live release rate).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Stray animal picked up by RCDAS Field Services.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Stray brought to shelter by member of the public (Over the Counter).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal brought into the clinic for spay/neuter surgery that RCDAS had to keep overnight, usually due to medical complications.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: TNR cat brought into the clinic for spay/neuter surgery that RCDAS had to keep overnight.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal transferred into RCDAS possession from another organization or another RCDAS shelter.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal transferred from contract city that has animal control but not sheltering. Example: Desert Hot Springs transfers animals to CVAC.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal transferred internally between RCDAS shelters (Blythe, CVAC, Jurupa Valley, San Jacinto). Example: Blythe transfers to CVAC for more visibility and live outcome opportunities.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal custody permanently transferred to an adopter at an adoption event. Use when tracking adoptions for a specific event or grant reimbursement. Subtypes: CONTRACT, NEW, SURGWAIT (same meanings as ADOPTION subtypes).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal custody permanently transferred from RCDAS to an individual adopter through the adoption process.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal adopted unaltered where a spay/neuter deposit has been paid.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: General adoption (most common adoption type).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal adopted at offsite location (not Petco, PetSmart, or adoption event).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: RCDAS animal housed at Petco location was adopted.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: RCDAS animal housed at PetSmart location was adopted.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal adopted and in shelter awaiting surgery before going home.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Community cat: any free-roaming cat that may be cared for by residents of the immediate area, known or unknown; may or may not be feral. Impounded cats spay/neutered, eartipped, and released. Counted as intake and outcome.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)
notes: Same note as TNR

definition: Animal died unassisted while in RCDAS custody.
synonyms: died in kennel; death in care
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Died during treatment or recovery at an offsite vet.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Died on the way to or from the shelter, clinic, or another facility.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Died while placed in a foster home.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Found deceased in kennel while in shelter care.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Died during a surgical procedure.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Died and was submitted for rabies testing.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal euthanized while in RCDAS custody, for medical, behavioral, or legal reasons.
synonyms: put down; put to sleep; PTS; euthanized
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Euthanized while at a veterinary office (e.g., after-hours emergency vet during standby).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Euthanized under directive from a court or legal authority, typically following a ruling on public safety or dangerous dog designation.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Owner requests humane euthanasia due to medical or behavioral issue (e.g., senior pet with chronic illness and poor quality of life).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Euthanized due to suspected rabies infection and submitted for FRA testing to confirm presence of the rabies virus.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Humanely euthanized because too young to survive or thrive alone, lacking a mother or suitable care option (e.g., newborn kittens without foster opportunity).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Euthanized due to behavior issues that could have been improved or resolved in a typical home but were not manageable within the shelter setting.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Euthanized due to medical conditions that are treatable but cost-prohibitive or beyond shelter resources (e.g., treatable URI requiring resources the shelter cannot provide).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Euthanized due to severe behavior issues considered unsafe or untreatable even with intervention (e.g., formally declared dangerous/vicious through legal process).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Euthanized due to serious or terminal medical conditions that are not treatable and significantly impact quality of life.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal outcome is unknown. Subtypes: ESCAPED (physically escaped from shelter), IN FOSTER (lost while in foster care), IN KENNEL (unaccounted for in shelter, no evidence of escape), STOLEN (stolen from RCDAS custody).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Community cat impounded and released back into the field; CCP CAT and EARTIPPED variants identify community cats. Lindsay recommends these subtypes become obsolete once the new Community Cat SOP is complete.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Shelter-based program for managing community cats. Healthy cats with no signs of ownership are sterilized, vaccinated, ear-tipped, and returned to the location found. Counted as intake and outcome.
synonyms: return to field; shelter neuter return
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)
notes: Same note as TNR

definition: Animal returned to its owner after being processed as an intake. Use when the original owner reclaims their animal.
synonyms: reclaim; redemption; owner pickup; get my dog back; get my cat back
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Billing purposes only: known owner charged for time animal remained in custody after failing to reclaim (NOT counted in live release rate).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Owner changed their mind about reclaiming after starting the return-to-owner process.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal returned to owner unaltered where a spay/neuter deposit has been paid.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal returned to owner at RCDAS shelter.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal returned to owner in the field by Field Services officer (e.g., microchip verified, returned to owner's home).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal RTO'd and in shelter waiting on surgery.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Community-based practice, distinct from shelter-based RTF, primarily for managing feral cat populations. Cats are humanely trapped, sterilized, vaccinated, ear-tipped, and returned to their original outdoor location, often under a community caregiver. NOT counted as intake or outcome.
synonyms: trap neuter return; community cat surgery
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)
notes: Lindsay: once Community Cat SOP is complete, TNR/CCP/RTF should be the three main cat outcome types

definition: Animal custody transitioned to another organization or another RCDAS shelter.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Transferred to another animal control agency.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Transferred to Animal Samaritans (animalsamaritans.org).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Transferred to veterinarian for medical care (e.g., offsite emergency clinic).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Transferred unaltered where a spay/neuter deposit has been paid.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Transferred internally between RCDAS shelters (NOT counted in live release rate).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Transferred to Animal Friends of the Valley (AFV); e.g., animals impounded in JPA (Joint Powers Authority) areas.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Transferred to offsite location (not PetSmart or Petco).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Transferred to PetSmart location for adoption exposure.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Being held for rescue pickup; rescue partner committed but animal still in shelter.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Transferred to rescue organization.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Transferred to Mary S. Roberts (MSR).
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

term: ADOPTION - TRIAL / FURSTDATE / FIELDTRIP; ADOPT EVNT - FURSTDATE
definition: Incomplete adoption pending spay/neuter surgery; replaced by Foster to Adopt.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)
notes: Discontinued: February 2025. Do not use for new records; reference for historical data only.

term: FOSTER / FOSTER - BITE / FOSTER - FURSTDATE / FOSTER - RETURN (kennel window)
definition: Used to track animals in foster via Kennel Window; replaced by the Foster Window.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)
notes: Discontinued: February 2025. Do not use for new records; reference for historical data only.

definition: Purpose unknown.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)
notes: Discontinued: Unknown. Do not use for new records; reference for historical data only.

definition: Transfers to rescue; replaced by Transfer/Rescue.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)
notes: Discontinued: Unknown. Do not use for new records; reference for historical data only.

definition: Foster/Field Trip return; now lives in the foster window.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)
notes: Discontinued: January 2025. Do not use for new records; reference for historical data only.

definition: Animal previously outcomed as Foster/Furstdate returned to shelter; some may remain in kennel window.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)
notes: Discontinued: January 2025. Do not use for new records; reference for historical data only.

definition: Purpose unknown.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)
notes: Discontinued: Unknown. Do not use for new records; reference for historical data only.

definition: COVID-era online appointment reclaim; walk-in reclaim resumed.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)
notes: Discontinued: Unknown. Do not use for new records; reference for historical data only.

term: STRAY - NIGHT / OWNER SUR - NIGHT / EUTH REQ - NIGHT / OWNER SUR - NIGHT/SPCA
definition: Intake codes used when night drop boxes were open. Night drop boxes are closed for animal welfare reasons.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)
notes: Discontinued: Unknown. Do not use for new records; reference for historical data only.

definition: Purpose unknown.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)
notes: Discontinued: Unknown. Do not use for new records; reference for historical data only.

definition: Used when San Jacinto shelter was closed; RCDAS no longer contracts with Ramona Humane.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)
notes: Discontinued: Unknown. Do not use for new records; reference for historical data only.

definition: Animals transferred in from Riverside Humane Mary S. Roberts; no longer occurs.
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)
notes: Discontinued: Unknown. Do not use for new records; reference for historical data only.

definition: Total animals entering the shelter minus transfers in from other agencies. Used when aggregating regional data to prevent double counting.
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: How long animals currently in care have been there. Calculation: current date minus intake date. Only includes animals without an outcome yet.
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: Foster caregiver providing end-of-life palliative care at home to an animal with a life-threatening condition. Fospice animals remain in the organization's custody.
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: Temporary care provided in a household setting. Not an outcome: fostered animals remain in the organization's custody until an outcome occurs.
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: Animal brought in by an animal control officer or law enforcement.
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: Is owner present? YES + voluntary = OWNER SUR. YES + forced = CONFISCATE. NO = STRAY or TRANSFER.
synonyms: which intake type do I use
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: How long animals stay in care before an outcome. Calculation: date of outcome minus date of intake. Only includes animals with an outcome; use averages or medians at aggregate level.
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: Organization that can choose which animals to accept.
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: Percentage of animals leaving the shelter through live outcomes. Correct intake/outcome coding ensures accurate calculation; internal DAS transfers, dead-on-arrival strays, and billing-only codes are excluded.
synonyms: LRR; save rate; live outcome percentage
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: Coordinating shelter intake to prioritize animals that most urgently need to enter. A consent-based process using intake assessment with finders and caretakers, in partnership with the community, to prevent overcrowding and keep pets in homes when in the best interest of animal and caregiver.
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: Organization required by government contract to accept all animals from a service area.
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: Who is taking custody? Original owner = RTO. New owner = ADOPTION. Organization = TRANSFER. Deceased = DIED or EUTH.
synonyms: which outcome type do I use
source: RCDAS Data Glossary v1.0 (Cicconi)

definition: Animal brought in by a community member (stray or owner relinquishment).
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: Pet relinquished by its owner with the intention of euthanasia; can be an intake or outcome type. Also called owner-requested euthanasia (ORE).
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: Animal in the organization's custody but unavailable for adoption or transfer, intended to live out its life in the organization's care (e.g., office cat).
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: Percentage of total intakes that have had an outcome. Calculation: total outcomes / total intakes. Shows whether the shelter population is remaining in balance.
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: Percentage of animals entering an organization that do NOT leave by a non-live outcome. Calculation: (Total Intakes - Non-live Outcomes) / Total Intakes. Non-live outcomes include died in care, lost in care, shelter euthanasia, and owner-intended euthanasia.
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: The legally mandated period a stray animal must be kept in custody so the owner has an opportunity to reclaim. During the hold, the animal cannot experience any outcome.
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: Total animals entering the shelter, including transfers between organizations.
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: Aggregate count of animals leaving care through live placement: adoption, return to owner, return to field, transfers out, other live outcomes.
source: SAC Glossary (Outcomes for Pets / Shelter Animals Count)

definition: Euthanasia-related activity code (per Lindsay: 137=EUTH)
source: Call Center New Staff Reference Guide (draft)
notes: Code meanings pending confirmation with James Huffman (per Call Center guide comments)

definition: Penal Code 597 animal cruelty activity
source: Call Center New Staff Reference Guide (draft)
notes: Code meanings pending confirmation with James Huffman (per Call Center guide comments)

definition: Animal Service Representative (also appears as activity code)
source: Call Center New Staff Reference Guide (draft)
notes: Code meanings pending confirmation with James Huffman (per Call Center guide comments)

definition: Bright - Alert - Responsive: animal intake condition
source: Call Center New Staff Reference Guide (draft)

definition: Community Cat Program
source: Call Center New Staff Reference Guide (draft)

definition: Dead on Arrival
source: Call Center New Staff Reference Guide (draft)

definition: Dangerous Restraint Order (context: potentially dangerous/dangerous animal restraint orders)
source: Call Center New Staff Reference Guide (draft)
notes: Code meanings pending confirmation with James Huffman (per Call Center guide comments)

definition: Unknown; likely Dangerous/Service-related order code
source: Call Center New Staff Reference Guide (draft)
notes: Code meanings pending confirmation with James Huffman (per Call Center guide comments)

definition: Fluorescent Rabies Antibody test (rabies testing)
source: Call Center New Staff Reference Guide (draft)

definition: Hit by Car
source: Call Center New Staff Reference Guide (draft)

definition: Over the Counter
source: Call Center New Staff Reference Guide (draft)

definition: Unknown
source: Call Center New Staff Reference Guide (draft)
notes: Code meanings pending confirmation with James Huffman (per Call Center guide comments)

definition: Field radio communication codes (10-codes, 11-codes, Code-1 through Code-9) and phonetic alphabet. Full list lives in SOP 200-29 Standard and Emergency Radio Policy, Appendix A.
The tag number Chameleon generates automatically when a tag/link record is stored with the tag number field left blank.
source: SOP: Processing Dangerous and Potentially Dangerous Restraint Orders in Chameleon and Follow-up
notes: Same term defined differently in multiple SOPs; reconcile in pass 2

definition: Emergency traffic. All radio traffic stops immediately except for the officer calling in.
source: SOP: Standard and Emergency Radio Policy and Procedure

definition: Resume normal radio traffic.
source: SOP: Standard and Emergency Radio Policy and Procedure

definition: The unique animal identification number assigned in Chameleon at or prior to intake.
source: SOP: Owner Surrender and Rehome Support Request Form: Dogs

definition: Handwritten kennel card notes: A.A. for animal aggressive, F.A. for food aggressive, N.O.D. for no other dog. Do not pull the dog; immediately see an Animal Care Technician.
source: SOP: Volunteer Guidelines for Supporting Animal Services in Riverside
notes: Same term defined differently in multiple SOPs; reconcile in pass 2

definition: Handwritten kennel card notes indicating the dog may not be pulled; immediately see an Animal Care Technician.
source: SOP: Volunteer Guidelines for Supporting Animal Services at the Coachella Valley and San Jacinto Valley Animal Campuses
notes: Same term defined differently in multiple SOPs; reconcile in pass 2

definition: Animal Aggressive.
source: Retired SOP (source team disbanded)
notes: Term was defined only in a retired SOP; confirm it is still in use and its current definition.

definition: An Animal Control Officer assigned to the command post to coordinate information from the Incident Commander to Animal Control units at the scene and to the Department of Animal Services.
source: SOP: Emergency Disaster Plan

definition: Animal Care Technician.
source: SOP: Coachella Valley Animal Campus (CVAC) Animal Care Technician (ACT) Duties; Volunteer Guidelines for Supporting Animal Services at the Coachella Valley and San Jacinto Valley Animal Campuses; Volunteer Guidelines for Supporting Animal Services in Riverside...

definition: First administrative citation issued ($100) for the year.
source: SOP: Completion of the Chameleon Citation/Violation Window

definition: Second administrative citation issued ($200) for the same offenses within the year of the ADM CITE1 violation date.
source: SOP: Completion of the Chameleon Citation/Violation Window

definition: Third administrative citation issued ($500) for the same offenses within the year of the ADM CITE1 violation date.
source: SOP: Completion of the Chameleon Citation/Violation Window

definition: A non-criminal ticket issued to an individual who does not correct violations of state mandates and local ordinances. It can be issued whether or not the individual is present, and may be posted on the property, served, or signed.
source: SOP: Issuance of Administrative Citations
notes: Same term defined differently in multiple SOPs; reconcile in pass 2

definition: A citation issued for violations of animal-related ordinances, which may be eligible for extension requests.
source: SOP: Citations, Violations, Collections, and Administrative Process Call Procedures
notes: Same term defined differently in multiple SOPs; reconcile in pass 2

definition: An animal that is eight weeks of age or older, healthy, and free of behavioral or temperamental characteristics that would pose a health or safety risk to the community. Adoptable animals are suitable for placement as companion animals (California Food and Agricultural Code Section 17005).
source: SOP: Adoption Process
notes: Same term defined differently in multiple SOPs; reconcile in pass 2

definition: A shelter animal placed in the adoptable category based on an assessment of the overall health, temperament, and behavior of the animal. The animal's age, breed, size, or color is not a factor. Per California Food and Agricultural Code Section 17005, adoptable animals are animals eight weeks of age or older that have manifested no sign of a behavioral or temperamental defect that could pose a health or safety risk or otherwise make the animal unsuitable for placement as a pet, and no sign of disease, injury, or congenital or hereditary condition that adversely affects the animal's health or is likely to in the future.
source: SOP: Adoption of Shelter Animals
notes: Same term defined differently in multiple SOPs; reconcile in pass 2

definition: A form (Attachment B) completed by prospective adopters that captures lifestyle information, housing, household composition, and experience with animals to support the matching process.
source: SOP: Adoption Process

definition: A colored collar placed on an adopted animal to indicate that the animal has been adopted and to identify the adopter.
source: SOP: Adoption Process

definition: A trained RCDAS staff member who conducts adoption interviews, matches animals with adopters, provides education on pet care and transition, and facilitates the adoption process.
source: SOP: Adoption Process

definition: A two-part certificate (customer copy and office copy) issued to an adopter who returns an adopted pet within 7 calendar days, entitling the adopter to adopt another pet of the same type within one year. definition: The animal's reproductive status. Also called sterilized/unsterilized or spayed/neutered/intact. May be updated during the stay.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: A more specific description of why the animal is in the organization's custody.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: The reported reason the animal is in the organization's custody. Possible reasons vary widely among shelters.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: A more specific description of why the animal is no longer in the organization's custody.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: The reported reason the animal is no longer in the organization's custody.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: The animal's primary and, if applicable, secondary breed, usually determined only by physical appearance and a breed guess.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: The reported or estimated birthdate of the animal, often a guess based on appearance and teeth.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: The animal's age group at intake.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)

definition: A collaborative, industry-led nonprofit that maintains The National Database of sheltered animal statistics, used for trends and benchmarking across the country. RCDAS reports data to SAC.
source: Shelter Animals Count Animal Welfare Glossary (Jan 2025)
